J1.basis: This site clearly pivoted from **InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (an AI meeting assistant) — the GSC export is almost entirely legacy InboxZap demand ... none of it is Lumina-relevant content
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot with three grounded axes (live/homepage = meeting assistant, GSC = legacy inbox demand, repo = stale InboxZap metadata) and frames every recommendation around that reality, including reclassifying the homepage.

J2.basis: 3900 impressions, 2 clicks, avg position 42.3 ... 301-redirect https://lumina.example/blog/post-1 ... [full per-asset table for all 13 URLs]
J2.rationale: Walks the legacy inventory asset-by-asset with real GSC impressions/clicks/positions and assigns an explicit disposition, plus a justified manual override on the homepage row; dinged slightly because all 12 posts collapse to a single 'redirect' verdict with no harvest/keep/sunset differentiation among them.

J3.basis: (1) fix ... protect what little equity exists; (2) build the forward ai-meeting-assistant cluster ...; (3) re-audit and watch whether the redirected legacy pages hold position while the new cluster ramps
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — preserves salvageable equity via 301s (explicitly refusing to rewrite inbox-zero posts as meeting-notes) while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, and sequences the transition; only softness is that redirect targets default to the homepage for now.
